ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the customer */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the customer */,
  "company" TEXT /* Company name of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "phone_landline" TEXT /* Landline phone number of the customer */,
  "phone_mobile" TEXT /* Mobile phone number of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "category" INTEGER /* Category of the customer (foreign key to categories) */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the customer */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the customer record */,
  "date_created" TEXT /* Date the customer record was created */,
  "date_updated" TEXT /* Date the customer record was last updated */,
  "modified_by" TEXT /* User who last modified the customer record */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the customer (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "segment" TEXT /* Segment of the customer (e.g., small business, enterprise) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customers_category FOREIGN KEY ("category") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category" INTEGER /* Parent category (foreign key to categories) */,
  "active_flag" INTEGER /* Indicates if the category is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., sales representative, customer support agent, marketing team) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "active_flag" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user is active */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the user record */,
  "date_created" TEXT /* Date the user record was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interactions" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interaction */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer involved in the interaction */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who initiated the interaction */,
  "interaction_type" TEXT /* Type of interaction (e.g., meeting, call, email) */,
  "interaction_date" TEXT /* Date of the interaction */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes about the interaction */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the interaction */,
  "duration" TEXT /* Duration of the interaction */,
  "outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the interaction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interactions_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interactions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tasks" (
  "task_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each task */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer related to the task */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user assigned to the task */,
  "task_type" TEXT /* Type of task (e.g., follow up, meeting, email) */,
  "due_date" TEXT /* Due date for the task */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the task (e.g., pending, completed) */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority of the task (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "assigned_by" TEXT /* User who assigned the task */,
  "completed_by" TEXT /* User who completed the task */,
  "completion_date" TEXT /* Date the task was completed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("task_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tasks_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tasks_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "task_assignments" (
  "assignment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each assignment */,
  "task_id" INTEGER /* ID of the task being assigned */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user being assigned the task */,
  "assignment_date" TEXT /* Date the task was assigned */,
  "reassigned_by" TEXT /* User who reassigned the task */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_task_assignments_task_id FOREIGN KEY ("task_id") REFERENCES tasks ("task_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_task_assignments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "task_comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "task_id" INTEGER /* ID of the task being commented on */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the comment */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  "comment_date" TEXT /* Date the comment was made */,
  "parent_comment" INTEGER /* ID of the parent comment (foreign key to task_comments) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_task_comments_task_id FOREIGN KEY ("task_id") REFERENCES tasks ("task_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_task_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_task_comments_parent_comment FOREIGN KEY ("parent_comment") REFERENCES task_comments ("comment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "opportunities" (
  "opportunity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each opportunity */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer related to the opportunity */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user managing the opportunity */,
  "opportunity_type" TEXT /* Type of opportunity (e.g., sales, partnership) */,
  "opportunity_date" TEXT /* Date the opportunity was created */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the opportunity (e.g., pending, won, lost) */,
  "expected_revenue" REAL /* Expected revenue from the opportunity */,
  "probability" REAL /* Probability of winning the opportunity */,
  "close_date" TEXT /* Expected close date of the opportunity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("opportunity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_opportunities_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_opportunities_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "opportunity_comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "opportunity_id" INTEGER /* ID of the opportunity being commented on */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the comment */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  "comment_date" TEXT /* Date the comment was made */,
  "parent_comment" INTEGER /* ID of the parent comment (foreign key to opportunity_comments) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_opportunity_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_opportunity_comments_opportunity_id FOREIGN KEY ("opportunity_id") REFERENCES opportunities ("opportunity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_opportunity_comments_parent_comment FOREIGN KEY ("parent_comment") REFERENCES opportunity_comments ("comment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "activity_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer related to the activity */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who initiated the activity */,
  "activity_type" TEXT /* Type of activity (e.g., email, call, meeting) */,
  "activity_date" TEXT /* Date of the activity */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes about the activity */,
  "duration" TEXT /* Duration of the activity */,
  "outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_activity_logs_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_activity_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytic entry */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer related to the analytic */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user related to the analytic */,
  "metric" TEXT /* Type of metric (e.g., sales revenue, customer satisfaction) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the analytic was recorded */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the analytic data (e.g., CRM, marketing automation) */,
  "segment" TEXT /* Segment of the analytic data (e.g., small business, enterprise) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
